evals.prompt: Explain this file to me: models/user.py
evals.expected_output: 4-layer explanation: Context (what it does, where it fits), Data Flow (inputs/outputs), Details (key sections), Pitfalls (edge cases, gotchas)
expectations: Reads the target file and its immediate dependencies
Starts with Context layer — plain-language summary and architectural role
Includes Data Flow layer — what goes in, what comes out
Defines technical terms on first use
Suggests related files to explore next

evals.prompt: How does the authentication system work in this project?
evals.expected_output: Module-level explanation covering auth components, data flow, and connections between files
expectations: Explains at module scope: purpose, file listing with roles, internal dependencies
Generates a mini-diagram if 3+ components interact
Uses concrete examples from actual code

evals.prompt: Walk me through the create_deal function
evals.expected_output: Function-level explanation with signature, parameters, return value, side effects, and edge cases
expectations: Greps to find the function definition
Explains at function scope: signature, purpose, parameters, return value
Traces data flow through the function

evals.expected_output: Asks user to verify path instead of hallucinating
expectations: Does not hallucinate file contents or code explanation
Asks user to check the path or provide the correct one
Suggests using Glob to find similar files
